author: Vitor
aiExplanation: This function processes an input string character-by-character to build a new string based on special command symbols. Regular letters are normally added to the end of the text, but encountering a percent sign (`%`) toggles a "reversed" mode that changes how the string is modified: in the normal state, new letters are added to the end and an asterisk (`*`) deletes the last letter, whereas in the reversed state, new letters are added to the front and an asterisk deletes the first letter. Additionally, encountering a hashtag (`#`) duplicates the entire accumulated text, and if the process finishes while still in the "reversed" state, the function reverses the final string one last time before returning it.

author: Vitor
notes: Solution using an array without actual reversing.
aiExplanation: This JavaScript function processes an input string character by character to build a new text string, using three special symbols to modify the text as it is being created. Normal characters are simply added to the end of the text, but encountering a percent sign (`%`) toggles a "reverse mode" that flips this behavior, causing new characters to be added to the front instead. An asterisk (`*`) acts like a backspace, deleting the most recently added character (the last character in normal mode, or the first character in reverse mode), while a hashtag (`#`) instantly duplicates the entire accumulated text. After reading all characters, the function combines them into a single string—reversing the entire sequence at the very end if "reverse mode" is still active—and returns the final result.

author: Vitor
aiExplanation: This JavaScript function processes an input string character by character to build a new string based on specific commands. As it loops through the text, it adds regular letters to an accumulating list, but if it encounters a special symbol, it performs a unique action: an asterisk (`*`) acts like a backspace to delete the last character, a hashtag (`#`) duplicates all the text created up to that point, and a percent sign (`%`) reverses the order of the current text. Once it finishes reading the entire input string, it joins all the processed characters back together and returns the final modified text.
